title: lupinus lyallii subsp. washoensis
intro: Lupinus lyallii subsp. washoensis is a subspecies of lupine plant, specifically a type of wildflower native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating kidney and bladder issues, due to its high concentration of alkaloids such as lupanine and sparteine. The health benefits of Lupinus lyallii subsp. washoensis include reducing inflammation and improving overall urinary tract health, making it a valuable remedy for those suffering from conditions like kidney stones and urinary tract infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
